The correct augmented sixth chord with B as the root must include an augmented sixth interval above B, which is G𝄪 (enharmonically A♭), and typically also the major third above B, which is D♯. Option D, B–D♯–G, uses G natural instead of G𝄪, so it is not correct. Option A, F𝄪–B–D♯, and option B, D♯–F𝄪–B, both contain the correct notes but with F𝄪 as the bass in A, while B has the correct root position with B as the lowest note.  

\boxed{B}
